text:
HSN Community / Forums / HSN Arcade / Can you beat 22,375 on Mahjongg Dimensions?
Can you beat 22,375 on Mahjongg Dimensions?
This week's Game of the Week challenge for Friday Night Game Night is to beat my score of 22,375 points on Mahjongg Dimensions!
Anyone with scores equal or better than mine from Friday, October 7 to Thursday, October 13 at 11:59PM ET will be entered for a chance to win a $250 shopping spree!
Come back to this Community thread during Friday Night Game Night from 8PM – 11PM ET to see if you've won and to check out next week's Game of the Week!
Post your scores and comments below!
Congratulations to our 10/14/2016 winner!
okay this week i was lucky enough to hit 25350. what i want ot know is how do the other scores get sooooooo high....
Ok, I scored 22,450, which beats her score.
But I love playing these games, for almost 4 years now, but I have never won anything.
Just fun I guess to share!
All have a good night!

Discussion in 'SUS VENATOR CLUB' started by Bama, Mar 7, 2018.
Did some stalk hunting going from field to field.
This field usually has a bunch of hogs at the feeder and is accessible without spooking the hogs if at feeder.
I estimate a dozen hogs in field with this big boar watching them feed.
He was standing about 20 yds from feeder just watching the other hogs eat or sizing up which sow he wanted a date with?
Walked far enough in to clear shooting house and set up sticks.
Shooting a 300 BO with Barnes Bullets 110 gr TAC-TX hand load.
I shot drop right there.
Gun jammed after third shot.
May have hit another hog running but did not have too much time to look.
This was about a 75 yard shot using a Pulsar Trail XP 50 with DLOC mount.
I'll post video in hunting section.
lonepunman, Juanthelawnmowerthief, Duffy and 7 others like this.
Looks like a set of double tusks on that boar.. Congrats on the Sus boar - and trophy choppers too!
30 Herrett - The best all out terminal performance on hogs and deer out to 300yds the 30 Herrett is it.
The round hits like Thor's hammer, DRT.
A perfect match for Texas hog hunters.
The 6.8 SPC is the fastest growing and #1 choice for hunting deer and hogs with an AR15.
War Eagle or Roll Tide?
Figure it's the latter with 'Bama'.
Nice beast dang I can smell the stench from here big ol feller.
FYI not sure if the malfunction is a rarity with your 300, but if it's a regular thing I have about 250 rounds through the RNZ Unimag and it's fantastic so far.
The anti-tilt follower is quite ingenious....I think...so far.
Tilts to the rear so the front of the follower can't dip down.
1st round to last loads like a dream.
Anyway thought I'd bring it up my buddy found them researching for CProducts mags.
Took a chance...liking them so far.
They take 300bo, 6.5, both flavors of x39, 450, .223...all in one.
Congrats on that big ol' boy.
Many thanks 437!
I'll give that a try.
I went back to the lodge and cleaned the 300 BO real well thinking that might be a problem.
I've been shooting it quite a bit in last couple weeks and kinda lost track of cleaning.
Also, I filed down the rail on inside of .223 mag.
Saw on Utube that helps line them up better.
Thanks for the heads up and I'll give RNZ Unimag a try.
It looked like the ground shook when he fell.
Yea it may have just been gunked up.
Went out last night and got SKUNKED.....I mean total.
I did not see a deer, possum, raccoon, squirrel, armadillo....nada...obviously no yotes or hogs.
What a bizarre ghost town.
Thanks!
Need to get back out there and let some lead fly!
Just got a 6.8 SPC upper from Eric at Blackstone Arms and some 120 gr Cavity Back bullets.
Very nice boar!
Good going.
I like the way you marked the 300 mag.
I am going to do the same, don't want no problems!
I have an upper from Eric too.
I don't reload but would love to try those cavity backs.
He is working on adding the sale of loaded rounds in SPCII potency.
Keep your ear to the ground.
JoeBano - Magazine - Spray Painted with brown paint , let dry then white paint pen and stencil sheet from Hobby Lobby.
I too don't want a mishap!
Thanks and enjoy!
Big nasty ugly muggly down!
Congrats.
In the ecosystem thanks you... he pirated a lot of resources to get that big.
Good photos as well - they can be tough to get.

I was searching for a Property and found this listing (MLS® #3294232).
Please send me more information regarding 1900 E 7th Avenue Pkwy, Denver, CO, 80206.
Thank you!
I'd like to request a showing of 1900 E 7th Avenue Pkwy, Denver, CO, 80206 (MLS® #3294232).
Thank you!

Sadly, news just arrived of Australian composer Phillip Houghton's (1954-2017) passing this last October 1, 2017 (see: ).
This, not even a year after the untimely death of Roland Dyens (1955-2016) on October 29, 2016.
Each composer contributed greatly to the guitar's repertoire and here is an excerpt of their discography.
May their music live through the international guitar community.
As mentioned in Sounboard (June 2017, Volume 43 No.
2 p. 16), Roland was the highest (then) living composer in the TOP 100 with his Tango en skaï.
He was quite happy about this and asked me if he could tell, after reading the manuscript of GT 100 and accepting to endorse it.

David recently participated in the Ironman 70.3 Steelhead in Benton Harbor, Michigan.
An impressive challenge for any endurance athlete, the triathlon race format included a 1.2-mile swim, 56-mile bike, and 1⁄2 marathon (13.1 miles).
Way to go, David!

Whether your application calls for work with racks, machinery, or other materials and equipment, you cannot go wrong with the Standard RolastairTM Rolling Ladder.

13 step units and larger are provided with a folded base for ease and economy in shipping.

Our 725-BN is comparable to Moen's S6500BB faucet by its function and form.
Both faucets are covered by a lifetime warranty, and are ADA approved.
However, they differ in price.
Our faucet is priced at $97.00 compared to Moen's MSRP of $537.65.

It was nightmare!
SOHUVA_-C207715-NTI-NG-TP-S.EXE virus hijacked my PC!
I wrote this post to show you how to remove (uninstall& cleanup) SOHUVA_-C207715-NTI-NG-TP-S.EXE from your system and from browsers: Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Internet Explorer, Microsoft Edge.

A.
Load film into the tank in total black-out conditions.
Keep the loaded tank in your presence until step C.
B.
Prepare the process solutions and arrange all in sequence of usage from left to right.
Note: temperatures for all solutions ideally are between 68 – 70 °F.
The development time below is for a Developer temperature of 68 °F.
Please consult the Time/Temperature Chart for alternative times if temperature varies.
Developer – Ilfotec HC: 1 part concentrate + 31 parts water —> 15ml concentrate added to 500ml water.
Stir gently and set aside.
Wetting Agent – Kodak Photo-Flo: 2-3 drops concentrate added to 500ml water.
Stir VERY gently and set aside.
About six batches of 500ml (total 3L) of water for the final rinse and each wash sequence in steps 5 and 6.
8.
Dry .....
Very carefully, ensuring that other film in cabinet does not contact wet film ..... 2 hours – NOTE: Always open and close film drying cabinet slowly to minimize dust particles in the air.
9.
Clean up the area.
10.
Cut and file negatives ..... cut film strip into sections of 3 frames, file into sleeves in number sequence, label the page and store in negative storage binder.

We found around 2 Cockshutt C parts in our catalog.
You may refine your search based on selections on the right.
